chapter_title.en: Chapter 90 – The shade for the one in Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/302)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/302)
text: مَا رَأَيْتُ أَحَداً كَانَ أَشَدَّ تَشْدِيداً فِي الظِّلِّ مِنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) كَانَ يَأْمُرُ بِقَلْعِ الْقُبَّةِ وَ الْحَاجِبَيْنِ إِذَا أَحْرَمَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Ali Bin Al Rayyan, from Qasim Al Sayqal who said,

‘I did not see anyone who was more intense in strictness regarding the shade than Abu Ja’farasws. Heasws used to order with the dome of the tent and the two curtains (to be removed) when heasws wore Ihraam’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Rayyan from al-Qasim al-Sayqal who has said the following: “I did not see anyone more strict about shadow in the state of Ihram than abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, who would command to remove the dome (tent) and its poles when he assumed the state of Ihram.’”
